215773.explanation: Residents decide for themselves whether they want to make their home more sustainable and what budget they allocate for that.

215774.explanation: A beautiful area to organize great events.

215775.explanation: There must be enough women's toilets. There are practical guidelines for this. It is also desirable that men's and women's toilets are truly separate, so no urinals between the ladies' toilets.

215777.explanation: Special walls for graffiti art are harmless. It can prevent existing buildings from being decorated. It is also a form of expression.

215778.explanation: There is already policy in place here and we see that the SEC (Sports Education Center) has started various initiatives for the youth.

215779.explanation: Cultural and historical heritage must be well preserved for future generations. Clear agreements about the amounts are necessary.

215780.explanation: We prefer music in the classroom at every primary school instead of separate music lessons. Music shapes children and enriches their development.

215781.explanation: The subsidy is not well spent. Large institutions receive a lot of subsidy while small associations struggle to get by.

215782.explanation: We prefer more community officers on the street rather than extra camera surveillance.

215783.explanation: The municipality must ensure that meeting places are safe for everyone and not just for a small group.

215784.explanation: More police presence on the street to catch real criminals instead of more cameras. We do not want mass surveillance; privacy is highly valued by FVD.

215786.explanation: The police should be called in if a situation threatens to get out of hand.

215788.explanation: Events face all kinds of criteria and safety requirements; organizers already contribute to safety.

215789.explanation: If acute care is needed (for health reasons, for example), it makes no sense to wait until a house becomes available. We do find it worrying that there are so many homeless people and think that Dutch homeless should be assigned housing as quickly as possible.

215791.explanation: Many social rental homes are allocated to people with an urgency declaration, including many status holders. We see too little attention for residents in the middle segment. We would like to see more affordable owner-occupied homes and affordable private rental homes.

215792.explanation: We are not in favor of high-rise buildings or tower blocks. We find this does not fit the city's appearance.

215793.explanation: Property rights are being undermined again. And there are too few private rental homes available; this measure hinders the private housing supply.

215794.explanation: Only if that has no consequences for the companies already established there.

215796.explanation: We would prefer more customization here. If 30 percent of residents disagree, that is still a large percentage. We prefer focusing on renovation. Partial renovation and partial demolition is an option. It concerns someone's home.

215800.explanation: A reciprocal obligation for benefits? Then a job with a proper salary offers a more promising position for people. Moreover, staying completely at home is unhealthy; many people lose all connection with society. Of course, protected workplaces will be necessary.

215801.explanation: If the municipality sticks to its core tasks and works more efficiently, taxes for everyone can be lowered.

215803.explanation: We even want more free parking spaces. The recent price hikes may be the final blow for many entrepreneurs.

215805.explanation: And additional free parking spaces before 6:00 pm and 9:00 pm.

215807.explanation: People with physical disabilities already face enough obstacles daily and often have a tighter budget.

215808.explanation: There should be more car-friendly streets.

215810.explanation: Owning a car means more autonomy.

215812.explanation: More bicycle paths but not at the expense of car roads, no unnecessary and dangerous road narrowings.

215813.explanation: If many labor migrants become homeless, we believe the companies that have benefited from these labor migrants should help and shelter these people; we do not see this as a municipal responsibility. If Dutch children and adults end up on the street, then the municipality must intervene. In our view, the municipality is primarily responsible for its own residents.

215814.explanation: Trust in youth care is low because of many abuses within youth care. But it is unclear what is meant by 'stricter rules.'

215816.explanation: Leave the responsibility primarily with the parents.

215751.explanation: Within the scenarios calculated by the municipality, residents have the opportunity to indicate a preferred variant. This way, decisions are made, where possible, by the people directly affected. The condition is that variants must be feasible, affordable, and executable.

215752.explanation: We want to introduce a citizen's assembly in Oss: a representative group of residents invited by the municipality to think along about major issues, such as sustainability, housing, or poverty. The recommendations from this assembly will be made public and constitute an official advice to the municipal council.

215754.explanation: Festivals and events belong to a lively municipality. They bring residents together. We just need to carefully consider under what conditions we find this acceptable. Sociaal Sterk Oss does not want to compromise on noise at events, as this harms nature and the support of local residents.

215755.explanation: Permit-free construction should be possible in some cases, such as for family care homes to allow a family member in need of assistance to live close by. We can establish simple rules for this, which can be checked afterwards.

215756.explanation: Despite the shortage of good housing, many buildings remain vacant. Sociaal Sterk Oss finds this a disgrace, especially in these times. Therefore, we want an effective vacancy policy. It's no coincidence that the government has allowed permits for habitation.

215757.explanation: Business parks are nearly full and there is no room for new ones. We must therefore be critical: what do these companies add to our municipality besides employment and income? Sustainability could be a requirement for new companies.

215758.explanation: We do not find it necessary for cafés and restaurants to be open until four a.m. Opening hours can occasionally be extended if there is a reason for it. But we want to protect the living environment (residents, stakeholders).

215759.explanation: Politics is about making choices; we therefore do not cut care budgets but do look at expenses. Those dependent on care must be able to receive it. We consider 'what a resident can do, what a resident needs, and what the municipality can provide.' We pay for the cheapest suitable solution.

215760.explanation: We handle your tax money carefully. We support associations through subsidies to compensate them for rent. Furthermore, rent for associations is already lower than for commercial parties. However, we want to involve associations in how we manage neighborhood and village community centers.

215761.explanation: We want to support entrepreneurs with a low-interest loan to become more sustainable. Because entrepreneurs can sustainably invest under very favorable conditions and the loan is repaid, this form of financing pays for itself.

215762.explanation: We do not commit to having at least two pitches. This can be more or less, depending on the location and needs. It is top-down to determine this from the municipality. We prefer to engage in dialogue with caravan dwellers and the surroundings to develop plans together.

215763.explanation: This question is incorrect. Refugees with a residence permit are status holders. We must be honest about the housing crisis. It is not caused by refugees but by too few homes. So, build! We grant priority based on the most urgent situations.

215766.explanation: The Dutch language is a requirement to fully participate in society. For those settling in the Netherlands, we expect them to learn the language. For tourists, some information can be offered in another language.

215767.explanation: Companies must stop offering accommodations; it makes labor migrants vulnerable; exploitation and unfair competition are risks. We want companies to contribute via a fund to housing for labor migrants. We want to locate them in central locations.

215768.explanation: We do not want to expand the events policy. But we can imagine that established annual events receive a simplified permitting procedure from the municipality. This helps organizers and reduces administrative burden.

215769.explanation: The municipality cannot do this either. For example, owners of national monuments are exempt.

215770.explanation: We find this stigmatizing. It makes it visible directly who is poor and who is not. We find it more important to keep associations, sports, and culture accessible. Subsidies keep entrance fees low, 'participation is important' provides extra support, and diverse programming lowers barriers.

215771.explanation: More flower-filled beds will be placed in public spaces. Where mowing is necessary, it will be done in phases. Residents may maintain municipal green areas and receive a budget for this, provided that biodiversity is promoted instead of monoculture.

215772.explanation: We give farmers more space to sell products directly from their farms. We support cooperatives and social enterprises that promote local products, shorten supply chains, and consider people and the environment. This way, you can maintain with fewer livestock.

215773.explanation: We want to stimulate the sustainability of homes through very attractive loans for sustainable improvements.

215774.explanation: From a survey we conducted among stakeholders, there is little to no support for expanding the number of events at Geffense Plas. This is harmful to nature and causes more nuisance to those involved.

215775.explanation: Sufficient toilets must be available, including for men/women and differently-abled residents.

215776.explanation: Yes, where possible, we should do this. Alcohol is harmful to health and causes much damage to society. Scientific research also shows that children and young adults are especially sensitive to such advertising.

215777.explanation: We welcome creative expressions in public spaces. This can help with that. Additionally, it prevents vandalism in places where graffiti is not desired.

215778.explanation: One in three households is at risk of poverty. Children should certainly not suffer from this; in our opinion, every child should be a member of an association. As a municipality, we encourage membership and raise awareness among parents and children. We fully support the 'participation is important' scheme.

215779.explanation: Old buildings, stories, and traces underground tell us where we come from. In these times, many changes occur, and the monuments of the future are being made. We believe that our heritage must be preserved for the future. This we organize together with owners and other governments.

215780.explanation: As a municipality, we stimulate membership and raise awareness among parents and children. Our goal is that across the entire municipality of Oss, there is sufficient choice among sports, culture, and other leisure associations. We want to stimulate this particularly up to the age of 18. These are the artists of the future.

215781.explanation: We do not assess cultural facilities purely on economic returns but on their added value; a cultural facility is not a hospitality establishment and offers more than food and drinks. Oss spends relatively little on cultural institutions compared to other municipalities.

215782.explanation: We deploy neighborhood officers, neighborhood prevention teams, street coaches, and municipal enforcement officers in neighborhoods where this is necessary. We want to do this on a multi-year basis so networks can be built and maintained. We invest in increased supervision and enforcement.

215783.explanation: It is important that everyone feels safe in our municipality.

215784.explanation: We are not inherently against camera surveillance. Where it can help with investigations, preventing or reducing nuisance, camera surveillance is an option. We emphasize that we want to continue weighing the different interests (privacy, public order, safety, costs).

215786.explanation: We find it more important that our enforcers are visible and approachable in neighborhoods and districts. That they know the people and build long-term networks. By focusing on this, we strengthen the feeling of safety and trust in the local government.

215787.explanation: We do not want to further normalize drug use. Drug use is harmful to health, and behind the production and trade of drugs is a very well-organized criminal network. We want to reduce drug use and production, not encourage use at events.

215788.explanation: We consider events important; however, it cannot be that commercial profits benefit organizers while society bears the burdens. Police and municipal supervisors and enforcers' capacity can only be deployed once. We want to balance this better.

215789.explanation: In Scandinavian countries, this has proven to be very effective. The care and support costs for society are lower, people relapse less often and less quickly, and the wellbeing of (former) homeless people increases. Effective on all fronts, therefore.

215791.explanation: More than one-third of the homes must consist of social rental housing, half of mid-priced rental and affordable owner-occupied homes, and the remainder higher-priced owner-occupied and rental homes.

215792.explanation: More housing must be built. By building higher, we make it easier to create affordable rental and owner-occupied homes, including social rental housing. Of course, we must carefully consider where this is suitable and desirable.

215793.explanation: Homes are not commodities. Therefore, we believe homes should be lived in by the owner. This also prevents investors from buying up entire streets and renting them out at excessively high rents. This also breaks down communities.

215794.explanation: Business parks are called business parks for a reason. In most cases, it is undesirable because there is higher noise, odor, or emissions. This does not fit within a residential environment.

215796.explanation: In principle, we are in favor of tenant participation. However, a housing corporation and a municipality serve the public interest, which can be at odds. Marginalizing tenants is not acceptable; the 70% norm results in little to no new homes. We advocate for a local model.

215798.explanation: Room-by-room renting can contribute to adding extra living spaces in municipalities. Think, for example, of student housing. We want this to be subject to a permit requirement so that we have a clear overview and can spread it across neighborhoods and districts.

215800.explanation: We do what is appropriate to guide people toward work. This may be socially meaningful work, but also training or courses. Sociaal Sterk Oss looks at what works, as people are different and so are their possibilities. Tailored solutions are more successful.

215801.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes prevents people from falling into debt and deeper problems. Prevention is better than cure. The government must show its humane side and use common sense. These go hand in hand here.

215803.explanation: We support the idea that short-term parking in the center should be more expensive than long-term parking on the outskirts. This makes visiting the center more pleasant for everyone by better regulating parking spaces. For certain groups, however, the higher rates have very negative effects. We will solve this.

215805.explanation: We want to come to an ideal distribution in consultation with center user groups. However, as far as we are concerned, those plans must be feasible, affordable, and executable. Parking revenues must cover the parking company.

215806.explanation: We believe that it is better to stimulate good behavior than to tax polluting behavior more heavily.

215807.explanation: People with physical disabilities do not always have the choice to use another mode of transport or to walk to their destination. They are therefore already forced to incur higher transportation costs; there is no freedom of choice here. We support free parking with a disabled parking permit.

215808.explanation: Those already exist in the form of bicycle streets.

215809.explanation: We want to develop a section up to and including the Oijensezij. We will also pay attention to sufficient greenery and input from local residents.

215810.explanation: Where possible, the municipality should do this. It is a sustainable alternative to the regular car. Sociaal Sterk Oss believes that all reasonable alternatives that contribute to a cleaner, more sustainable, and well-accessible municipality should be explored. The shared car can be helpful.

215811.explanation: We want to stimulate and make public transport accessible in all respects, not only for residents aged 67 or older.

215812.explanation: Sociaal Sterk Oss wants an expansion of the number of fast cycling routes to and from the city of Oss and its various neighborhoods. Also, good bicycle parking in Oss, towns, and villages. When redesigning roads where the speed limit is above 30 kilometers, we advocate for separate bicycle paths.

215813.explanation: We see an increase in the number of roofless and homeless people in our municipality. Especially in winter, shelters are often full. It is unacceptable that people are forced to sleep on the streets. Humanity is not an option but our conviction.

215814.explanation: Attention in youth care is focused on the entire family. The family is and remains the basis from which work is done; we also detect unsafe situations. Residents and family members must receive the care and support they need at that moment. This must not be a point of discussion.

215815.explanation: We want to link this to the implementation of poverty policy.

215816.explanation: According to scientific research, teaching a healthy lifestyle early has lifelong effects. This is thus a good investment; we prevent social costs and increase the health and wellbeing of (young) residents. We also focus on parents/caregivers.

215751.explanation: According to PVV Oss, a referendum should be used for every major dossier, especially when it affects many residents and their living environment. Wind turbines destroy our landscape and do not contribute to reliable and affordable energy supply.

215752.explanation: PVV Oss believes that the referendum is a much better instrument for important decisions. It is the most democratic and accessible form of participation for citizens.

215754.explanation: Oss must become the vibrant municipality it once was. More flexible noise standards make more events and possible expansions of existing events possible.

215755.explanation: The housing shortage is high, and construction must begin. However, care must be taken with neighbors and stakeholders.

215756.explanation: PVV Oss thinks that owners themselves should be able to decide what they do with their property. Vacancy is not an abuse of ownership but a legitimate choice, for example, due to waiting for sale, redevelopment, or financial/legal reasons. Homeowners already pay enough property tax to the municipality.

215757.explanation: We want fewer rules and bureaucracy. Entrepreneurs must be given space without extra burdens or unnecessary obligations from the government.

215758.explanation: Our youth deserve a vibrant nightlife in the municipality. If hospitality entrepreneurs are allowed longer opening hours, there can be more offerings for young people. However, nuisances must be very limited.

215759.explanation: The municipality must above all ensure that our elderly receive good and accessible care. There must be no cutbacks here; the Social Support Act (WMO) is not a budget leftover.

215760.explanation: We support the Oss community life and our culture in every possible way.

215761.explanation: If an entrepreneur finds 'sustainability' sensible or necessary, they can consider the investment themselves. No climate ideals or green incentives are needed, and certainly not tax money.

215762.explanation: There must be sufficient space for trailer parks (wohnwagen). If there is not enough space, additional pitches must be made available.

215763.explanation: Our own residents, especially young people, are getting stuck in the housing market. Status holders should therefore not be given priority. Our own residents must have priority.

215766.explanation: Municipal information should be in Dutch. That promotes integration. Those who refuse to learn Dutch choose not to participate here and are free to leave. PVV prioritizes Dutch people, not immigrants.

215767.explanation: PVV opposes large-scale labor migration and the nuisance it causes. Labor migrants are often a profit model. If the responsibility for housing lies with companies, the threshold to bring labor migrants to the Netherlands is higher and both their numbers and the nuisance decrease.

215768.explanation: Fairs and other local traditions must be protected and prioritized in terms of support.

215769.explanation: Gas or electricity is a choice for the people of Oss, not the municipality or the housing corporation.

215770.explanation: In this way, people with low incomes are encouraged to participate in the Oss community life and culture.

215771.explanation: A clean neighborhood invites people to keep it clean. Playgrounds and parks should be neatly maintained. However, the municipality can choose to plant some green spaces and verges with wild and field flowers. This requires less maintenance and is good for birds and bees.

215772.explanation: Farmers are entrepreneurs who must be able to determine their own business model.

215773.explanation: This money mainly goes to homeowners; tenants literally remain out in the cold. The green madness and the climate policy that swallows millions must be stopped.

215774.explanation: Oss must be vibrant and attractive through more recreation and conviviality. The Geffense Plas is a beautiful area that can compare with similar waters in the region and deserves to be used as such.

215775.explanation: This symbolic measure leads to extra unnecessary administrative burden. Good events have their affairs in order since otherwise guests will not return. There must be sufficient (public) toilets for every resident of our municipality.

215776.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with consumer choices. This patronizing attitude can be removed immediately.

215777.explanation: Graffiti is not art but invites further decay. Murals as part of projects are open for discussion. Vandalism in the form of graffiti is not.

215778.explanation: We advocate for a strong community life and a healthy and vital youth. This policy addresses two issues at once and supports the poorest families in a basic need such as sports.

215779.explanation: The historical heritage of Oss must be protected and restored to keep the city recognizable and Dutch. If this requires more money, it must be allocated accordingly.

215780.explanation: Schools should focus on basic skills such as arithmetic and language.

215781.explanation: Cultural institutions should be able to sustain themselves as much as possible.

215782.explanation: Visible and approachable, with a focus on preventing nuisances and minor annoyances in the neighborhoods, the center, the villages, and the outskirts. PVV also wants additional officers for our municipality.

215783.explanation: The municipality must be safe for its residents, period. There is no room for a hysterical woke agenda.

215784.explanation: The city center is terrorized by loitering youths and fatbikes. The chance of being caught is low. Camera surveillance enables enforcement. Furthermore, camera surveillance is needed at the central station to combat massive bicycle theft.

215786.explanation: Tackling nuisance is urgently necessary. However, special training must be completed. PVV also wants additional officers for our municipality.

215787.explanation: The government should not facilitate drug use.

215788.explanation: Organizing an event also means ensuring it can and does take place safely. Damage caused by rioters will be charged to them.

215789.explanation: If there is nuisance, conditions can be imposed on the housing, such as mandatory assistance.

215791.explanation: PVV believes there must be enough social rental housing AND affordable owner-occupied homes for all residents of the municipality of Oss.

215792.explanation: Start building immediately. Oss needs housing.

215793.explanation: Housing for our residents and youth is the goal. Private rentals, social rentals, and owner-occupied homes are all needed to solve the housing shortage. Excesses such as large groups of migrant workers in too-small homes must be addressed.

215794.explanation: If the interests of businesses are not harmed, accessibility is well arranged, and facilities can be easily realized, this should be possible on a modest scale, provided it can be safe.

215796.explanation: Housing corporations seek a larger margin. This is subordinate to the living comfort of current residents. PVV Oss opposes forced demolition or renovation.

215798.explanation: Residents of the municipality of Oss should be free to use their homes as they wish—within the rules. Strict rules must be established for the maximum number of migrant workers per home.

215800.explanation: Nationally, more than half of social assistance goes to non-Western immigrants. Benefits are not a free right: those who can participate must give something back. A mandatory counterperformance is needed to combat dependency and abuse.

215801.explanation: For those who truly cannot pay, debt relief must remain in place.

215803.explanation: Parking was already too expensive and has only become more absurd since October! Parking must return to the situation before October 2025. This also contributes to the attractiveness of the center for Oss residents and aligns with the vision of a regional function of the Oss center.

215805.explanation: Since October 2025, restaurants and supermarkets have suffered from a failing and short-sighted milk-out policy by the municipality, which affects the residents and visitors of the Oss center.

215806.explanation: PVV Oss is fundamentally against unfeasible symbolic policies such as environmental zones, for any type of traffic. People from outside Oss will never cycle to Oss because of an environmental zone.

215807.explanation: Going out with a physical disability is already a challenge. We as the municipality of Oss must do our best to help these people be as much a part of society as possible.

215808.explanation: Cars belong in and around a city. Good and proper access must ensure a good distribution of cars, not yet another way to annoy motorists.

215809.explanation: Oss must move towards a complete ring road where the Northern Ring Road is connected to the Gewandeweg and Heihoeksingel, to create full accessibility for traffic in all directions for all current and future Oss residents. Nuisance for local residents should be minimal.

215810.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with residents' transportation choices. Instead, the municipality should ensure good public transport in Oss and its villages.

215811.explanation: Free public transport does not exist: it must be funded through taxes. The municipality must ensure good connections and affordable public transport for everyone.

215812.explanation: Good and safe bike paths are important, but they should not come at the expense of roads for cars.

215813.explanation: Sending foreign homeless people back. More shelter locations attract extra nuisance and insecurity. The focus must be on preventing nuisance, strict enforcement, and appropriate care outside residential neighborhoods, so residents can feel safe again in their neighborhood.

215814.explanation: Children and young people who need care must receive it.

215815.explanation: The municipality can better help residents by lowering the burden for all inhabitants.

215816.explanation: A healthy lifestyle for youth is a task and responsibility of the caregivers. PVV Oss is against government paternalism.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more events at the Geffense Plas.

titleParts.text: Aan de Geffense Plas moet de gemeente meer

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.moreInfo: The Geffense Plas is a popular place for walking and sports. Sometimes events and festivals are organized there. Whether someone may organize an event depends on the number of visitors, the duration and the noise. Currently up to twenty events per year with more than 250 visitors are allowed.
statements.pro: Events provide liveliness, meeting opportunities and conviviality. The Geffense Plas is the best location for that. This boosts Oss’s profile and stimulates the local economy and tourism. Many young people in the municipality would like more events at the Geffense Plas.
statements.con: Local residents experience a lot of disturbance from loud events at the Geffense Plas. There are much better locations for events in Oss. The area around the Geffense Plas must not become overloaded; that is harmful to nature.

statements.title: Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to set up in Oss.
statements.moreInfo: Sustainable means that companies operate in a way that is good for people, the environment and the future. A sustainable company, for example, uses green electricity, reduces waste and reuses waste as much as possible, and takes account of neighbours and society. A sustainable company does not focus only on profit but prevents harm to nature and society.
statements.pro: Oss must also help counter resource depletion, pollution and global warming. To make our local economy as sustainable as possible, we may require companies to make their businesses more sustainable. Because business parks are almost full, we must be critical about new companies. Sustainability can be a requirement.
statements.con: This can cost investments and jobs. Sustainability should never be a reason to refuse companies. Not every company can immediately operate fully sustainably. We do not need local rules, because there are already sufficient national regulations.

statements.title: The municipality should ask residents by referendum for their opinion on the placement of wind turbines in Oss.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet inwoners via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

titleParts.text: om hun mening vragen over de plaatsing van

titleParts.glossary: Modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 261 meters high. The so-called hub height is 175 meters.

statements.moreInfo: With a referendum the municipality puts one subject to residents, for example a law or measure. Residents can then vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents of that municipality who are 18 years or older may vote. Both the municipal council and ordinary citizens can request a referendum. If citizens want a referendum they must collect signatures from others. The municipality’s rules state how many signatures are required. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding: an authority does not have to follow the outcome and can make a different decision.
statements.pro: Residents must have direct input here, because the wind turbines will remain in the Lithse polder for a long time. Residents should have more say over major changes, because those always have consequences for their wallets.
statements.con: The municipal council exists to make choices on these kinds of complex issues. Residents elect their representatives to the council. The municipal council has already decided that wind turbines may be installed.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: The national Housing Act (Huisvestingswet) stipulates that municipalities must provide housing for refugees with a residence permit. Municipalities may decide which groups receive priority for a social rental home. Priority often applies to people in medical emergency and to those who are at risk of becoming homeless. Some municipalities choose to give priority to refugees with a residence permit. Until they get a home these refugees live in an asylum seekers centre.
statements.pro: Priority for a social rental home is given to urgent and vulnerable housing seekers. Refugees with a residence permit are an urgent and vulnerable group.
statements.con: There is a housing shortage. The municipality should be more critical in assessing which housing applicants are urgent.

statements.title: The municipality should mow public grass and verges more often.
statements.moreInfo: For every patch of grass the municipality decides how often it is mowed. The municipality takes into account the use of the place, play areas, traffic, the soil and dog-walking spots.
statements.pro: Mown verges increase safety for traffic.
statements.con: Flowering verges are good for biodiversity, which is the assortment of plants, animals and insects in an area. Only in very untidy places is it necessary to mow more frequently.

statements.title: There should be an Oss pass that gives low-income people discounts on sports, school supplies and cultural activities. The municipality should pay for those discounts.
statements.moreInfo: In some municipalities people with a low income receive a pass that gives them access to various discounts. Oss does not have that yet. It does have the “Meedoen is belangrijk” scheme. People with a low income receive a contribution from this scheme for social, cultural and sports activities, school supplies and swimming lessons.
statements.pro: With such a pass all residents can participate in sports, culture and education regardless of income. Low-income people can thus take part in Oss’s club life and culture.
statements.con: Discounts on sports and school supplies for people on minimum incomes and other schemes already exist in Oss. Such a system creates extra bureaucracy and control. Such a card can be stigmatizing: it clearly shows who has a low income and who does not.

statements.title: Oss should establish a citizens' assembly for important decisions, to advise the municipal council.

titleParts.text: Oss moet bij belangrijke besluiten een

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

titleParts.text: instellen, dat advies geeft aan de gemeenteraad.

statements.moreInfo: For issues that have major consequences for residents the municipality can choose to hold a citizens’ assembly. A randomly selected group of residents then participates in the discussion about the plans and gives advice to the municipality. Oss does not have a citizens’ assembly; the municipality is investigating it.
statements.pro: A citizens’ assembly actively involves residents in local democracy. A citizens’ assembly is a fair way to take or prepare a decision. By lottery all interests are represented, including those of people who normally do not participate and young people.
statements.con: Everyone can already influence the municipal council through participation, the right to speak and voting. Decisions are made in the municipal council. A citizens' assembly should not replace that decision-making. There are already enough advisory councils, such as neighborhood and village councils, the youth council and the social domain advisory council.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible to build without a permit in certain locations.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het mogelijk maken om op sommige plekken zonder

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.moreInfo: For building and renovating an environmental permit from the municipality is usually required. Sometimes such a permit is not needed for smaller adjustments, for example for maintenance, a shed or a dormer window. There are not yet places where one can build completely permit-free.
statements.pro: Building without a permit ensures that more homes are delivered quickly and at lower cost.
statements.con: Rules are needed for safety and quality in order to build. Permits are necessary to take into account the people and nature around construction projects.

statements.title: At least two additional pitches should be provided for caravan/mobile-home residents.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Oss has a caravan (woonwagen) policy. It states that caravan dwellers are not treated differently from other residents, and that the municipality takes into account the location and the type of housing they are looking for.
statements.pro: Extra pitches provide certainty for caravan dwellers and ensure equal treatment. They have a great need for pitches. Caravan dwellers should be able to live in their own way. the caravan-dwelling culture is part of Oss.
statements.con: The municipality should not decide this from above. Depending on what caravan site residents want, there may be more or fewer pitches. This is unnecessary, because the current housing agenda already provides for 18 extra pitches. There is no need to add another two.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants should be allowed to remain open until four in the morning.
statements.moreInfo: Currently cafés and restaurants must close at 2:00 a.m. from Monday to Friday and at 3:00 a.m. on Saturday and Sunday. The municipality can grant permission to stay open longer or to close earlier.
statements.pro: This creates a lively city, especially for young people. With longer opening hours hospitality businesses have more freedom.
statements.con: People who live nearby are bothered when cafes and restaurants stay open later.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on the maintenance of monuments.
statements.moreInfo: Oss has about 300 municipal monuments. The municipality provides subsidies to maintain them. There are also national monuments, for which the national government is responsible.
statements.pro: Much heritage in Oss has already been demolished in the past. We must preserve cultural heritage for current and future generations. Monuments keep the city recognisable. If protection and restoration cost more, that money must be made available.
statements.con: There are enough subsidy schemes for monuments; no more money needs to go there. Owners of monuments are responsible for maintenance.

statements.title: The municipality may not force anyone to stop using natural gas.
statements.moreInfo: The national government has determined that from 2050 no natural gas will be used to heat buildings, heat water and cook. New construction must already be built without a gas connection. For existing homes municipalities must make neighbourhoods gas-free step by step. The municipality of Oss is working on a heat programme that will be adopted at the end of 2026. This programme will state which areas in Oss will switch and when.
statements.pro: New construction must already be gas-free. Making existing buildings mandatorily gas-free would infringe on freedom of choice and property rights. This is regulated nationally and is not a municipal task. The choice for gas or electricity is for the resident, not the municipality.
statements.con: Making homes gas-free makes them sustainable faster. Gas will eventually run out. Gas-free homes lead to lower energy bills. Gas consumption is more expensive than electricity, and you no longer have to pay annually for the gas connection.

statements.title: There should be more cycle paths, even at the expense of car roads.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality wants to encourage cycling. That could mean that more space for bike lanes is needed.
statements.pro: Cycling is healthy and environmentally friendly. It should become more attractive to cycle in our municipality. It is important that all population centers are accessible via safe and well-maintained cycling routes.
statements.con: There are sufficient cycle paths. We should primarily improve the existing infrastructure. The municipality should be easily accessible for both bikes and cars, without one being at the expense of the other.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure, with stricter rules, that fewer children and young people receive youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet er met strengere regels voor zorgen dat minder kinderen en jongeren

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities are responsible for youth care, for example help with family problems and with children's and young people's psychological and behavioral problems. The municipality is obliged to provide youth care but can decide which type of care and for how long.
statements.pro: Youth care costs are growing too fast. Choices are needed to provide care to those who really need it.
statements.con: Too many cuts have already been made to youth care. Vulnerable children and young people who need help must also receive support.

statements.title: New construction must include at least 40% social rental housing.

titleParts.glossary: Rental homes with a low rent (in 2026 this is an amount of at most € 932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Builders can earn the most from more expensive homes. But there is also great demand for cheaper homes and social rental housing. Therefore most municipalities ask developers to include at least a percentage of social rental homes. The national guideline for this is 30%. Oss now also requests 30% social rental housing in new developments.
statements.pro: Because far too few social rental homes have been built in recent years, more are needed now. Too much is being built in the more expensive segment because it yields higher returns. More social rental housing means more affordable homes for people who still have limited means.
statements.con: With too many social rental homes, fewer different types of people will live in neighborhoods. There should be more mid-rent homes (slightly higher rent than social housing) and affordable owner-occupied homes. More social rental homes can deter the investors who fund housing development. These homes yield lower returns.

statements.title: When issuing permits the municipality should give more room to local traditions and fairs at the expense of new events.

titleParts.glossary: Plural of "permit": permission from the municipality to do something.

titleParts.text: moet de gemeente meer ruimte bieden aan lokale tradities en kermissen ten koste van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.moreInfo: Events are regularly organized in Oss. Some events have a long tradition, such as the fair. Other events are new. To organize an event you must apply for a permit from the municipality. The municipality has rules for, for example, traffic, safety, distribution and noise around events. The municipality also makes sure there are not too many events on the same date and that events are well distributed.
statements.pro: Oss traditions and intangible heritage must be protected. They must not be replaced by new events.
statements.con: New events that keep Oss lively should also be given opportunities. They can coexist with local traditions and fairs.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.text: Elk kind op de basisschool moet met

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality subsidizes the project "Culture in the Classroom" and provides money to schools for music education. Low-income families can receive money for cultural activities, including music lessons. That does not yet apply to all children.
statements.pro: Music enriches children’s development. Low income should never be a reason that children cannot participate. Youth should be exposed more to music to preserve our traditions. We want to promote music lessons up to age 18. These are the artists of the future.
statements.con: Children receive enough music lessons at school. The municipality does not need to provide additional subsidies for that.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in the city centre of Oss.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: in de binnenstad van Oss komen.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Oss has cameras in public places in the city center and nightlife areas. Sometimes cameras are temporarily installed, for example at the fair or when incidents have occurred repeatedly, such as a fight. Camera surveillance is only permitted to maintain public order, detect crime and for traffic safety. Camera surveillance must comply with privacy legislation.
statements.pro: Camera surveillance can ensure that troublemakers are caught earlier. Camera surveillance keeps risk locations under extra observation. Safety in public spaces is very important.
statements.con: It is better to invest in social oversight, enforcement officers and good lighting. Camera surveillance is a symbolic measure that infringes on residents' privacy.

statements.title: In Oss, apartment buildings of more than eight storeys should be allowed.
statements.moreInfo: There are too few homes in the Netherlands and in the municipality of Oss. One way to build more homes is for the municipality to allow the construction of taller apartment blocks.
statements.pro: There is a housing shortage. Where it fits the surroundings, high-rise buildings help use city space more efficiently. Building higher creates more affordable rental and owner-occupied homes.
statements.con: There is enough space to build lower buildings and houses. People who live very high up have less natural contact with those on the street. They can become lonely or isolated.

statements.title: Parking in the centre of Oss should be free after 6 p.m., and on the Thursday shopping evening free after 9 p.m.
statements.moreInfo: From 1 October 2025 paid parking in Oss changed, with new zones, rates and payment times. Since then you must pay for a parking space in the city center after 6 p.m. and on the Thursday shopping evening after 9 p.m.
statements.pro: Free parking after 6 p.m. and on shopping evenings after 9 p.m. makes the center more welcoming and accessible.
statements.con: It is fair that people who go into town in the evening also pay for parking, just like during the day.

statements.title: The municipality should first help homeless people into housing and only then provide them with other assistance.
statements.moreInfo: The main problem for homeless people is that they have no home. But they often also have other problems. From 1 January 2025 the Verdihuis in Oss started a pilot "First a Home — youth" based on the Housing First principle. Housing First means: first a self-contained home without conditions, then other support services.
statements.pro: No one should sleep on the street. With a roof over their heads, homeless people gain rest and can work on other problems. This is an efficient way to resolve nuisance caused by homeless people. Costs for care and guidance decrease, people relapse less often and their wellbeing increases.
statements.con: Without first addressing other problems (addiction, debt or mental health issues), housing is likely to fail. Housing should be distributed fairly. That can be done by providing guidance and treatment to homeless people before they receive a home. That is too limited. The problems of homeless people must be tackled together and simultaneously.

statements.title: Oss should stop waiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.glossary: Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).
That people no longer have to pay for something.

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.moreInfo: If you cannot pay municipal taxes, you can ask the municipality for remission. That means you do not have to pay. The municipality looks at assets, income and expenses to assess whether someone qualifies.
statements.pro: There is already an anti-poverty policy. Those who cannot pay taxes can make use of it. It is better that people are self-reliant. By lowering taxes for some people, others would have to pay more.
statements.con: Debt forgiveness prevents people with debts from falling deeper into trouble. Debt relief helps reduce poverty and gives people more opportunities to participate in society. Ending debt forgiveness is inconsistent with the municipality's current anti-poverty policy.

statements.title: Associations should not have to pay rent to use neighbourhood and village community centres.
statements.moreInfo: Associations often use neighborhood and village halls. They pay rent for this.
statements.pro: This ensures that clubs are not pushed out by commercial parties who can pay more. By making community centers available, clubs remain low-threshold and accessible to everyone. Clubs are already struggling with excessively high costs.
statements.con: Associations often pay a reduced, social rate. It is reasonable that they pay rent for the space they use. Neighborhood and village halls need income from rent and hospitality to survive. There are subsidies that can compensate associations for part of the rent costs.

statements.title: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Oss should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa's) in Oss moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: BOAs work for the municipality and enforce the rules. For example, they hand out parking fines and monitor safety in nightlife areas. In Oss these officers currently do not carry a baton. The municipality can choose to equip BOAs with a baton.
statements.pro: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) must be well equipped. A baton reinforces their authority. With a baton BOAs feel safer.
statements.con: Only the police are allowed to use force.

statements.title: The municipality should pay for public transport for seniors aged 67 and over.
statements.moreInfo: Older people currently pay for public transport, like other travelers. Oss does have the Samen-Uitbus for adults aged 55 and over and people with a disability who want to go on an outing, and taxi transport for people with a Wmo transport pass. Travelers must pay a small contribution for these services.
statements.pro: This keeps elderly people mobile and independent.
statements.con: Older people are not necessarily poor. Public transport must be accessible to everyone.

statements.title: Cultural institutions should receive less funding.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

statements.moreInfo: Oss has a number of cultural institutions such as De Lievekamp, Groene Engel, the library and Muzelinck. These receive subsidies from the municipality. In addition they sell tickets for performances and students pay course fees. Cultural institutions also include all organizations committed to preserving, producing or presenting art, culture and heritage.
statements.pro: Cultural institutions must be entrepreneurial by seizing commercial opportunities. Cultural institutions must largely generate their own income as well.
statements.con: Cultural institutions are important for the Oss community, for example in terms of wellbeing. Cutting funding for them means many of these places will disappear. This makes culture less accessible to lower-income people.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute free tampons and sanitary pads at schools and community centers.
statements.moreInfo: Women and girls deal with menstruation for a large part of their lives. Most women in the Netherlands spend between €2.50 and €25 per month on tampons, sanitary pads or other menstrual products.
statements.pro: Menstrual poverty causes girls and women to sometimes miss school or work. By providing free menstrual products at schools and community centers they can participate.
statements.con: For those who cannot afford menstrual products, there are already enough schemes people can rely on. It is not the municipality's responsibility to pay for these products.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no paid work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. The municipality can require them to do socially useful work, for example as extra help at a school. That may not be work that would normally be paid. The municipality may (temporarily) reduce the social assistance benefit if someone refuses.
statements.pro: Socially meaningful work strengthens a person's self-confidence and provides work experience and social contacts. Receiving benefits is not without obligation: those who can participate must give something back. In this way people contribute to society.
statements.con: It is better to guide people. That can be socially useful work, but also training or a course. Tailored solutions are more successful. People are on welfare for different reasons, and mandatory work in exchange for benefits does not do justice to their situations.

statements.title: The municipality should provide safe meeting places for LGBTQIA+ people who need them.
statements.moreInfo: Oss is officially a Rainbow Municipality. Rainbow Municipalities work for the acceptance, emancipation and safety of the LGBTQIA+ community. LGBTQIA+ stands for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, intersex, asexual and more.
statements.pro: An inclusive municipality is one where everyone feels safe and accepted. A safe environment for this group is therefore necessary.
statements.con: Meeting places for specific target groups exclude other groups. The municipality should be a safe place for everyone, regardless of sexual orientation, origin and skin colour.

statements.title: The construction of the Northern Ring Road should go ahead.
statements.moreInfo: The Northern Bypass or access road in Oss must connect Kanaalstraat, Achterschaijkstraat, Spitsbergerweg and Frankenbeemdweg with the N329. The municipality wants to reduce traffic on Singel 1940-1945 and the surrounding streets. The plan is for a two-lane road with a maximum speed of 50 km/h. A separate cycle path will run alongside the road.
statements.pro: The Northern Ring Road makes the residential neighborhoods in Oss North more accessible. This relieves the city and makes through-traffic routes safer.
statements.con: Building the Northern Ring Road is too expensive. A new road invites more traffic. After a while it will be very busy here too.
